reviews.title: Rather not stay again.
reviews.text:
This is essentially an old Thai hotel that has been renovated by the Accor group. The worst aspect of this hotel is its location, right at the bottom (river end) of Silom Road, its location makes it extreamly difficult for transport although in saying that you can walk to Surasak or Saphan Tacsin BTS in about 15 – 20 minutes but be prepared to cross several busy main roads. Getting a taxi or tuk tuk is a real problem in rush hour and you must allow up to an hour to travel from Lumpini park end of Silom to the hotel by taxi at this time.
Check in and Check out are very inefficient, it’s not that the front desk don’t try their best but they are clearly understaffed with only two staff (both trying to deal with at least two customers each at any one time) on duty. Even though I had booked several days in advance I had a long wait for my room to be ready and this was arriving at 16:30.
The rooms themselves are very small and this is most notable in Bangkok where usually the cheapest hotels offer ample space. Not only are the rooms small but the design adds to the cramped and claustrophobic atmosphere. The bathrooms do not have baths, and the plastic integrated sink reminds me of the tired vanity units installed in the Thai first class railway compartments.
Don’t expect your bedding or towels to be changed frequently here, there is not even the usual “leave this card on the bed if you wish to change” option!
I’m not going to give this review any further of my time because frankly this hotel is just not worth it. Location, atmosphere, decoration, and service – this place just does not have anything positive to write about.

The only lights to keep the room illuminated were from the bedside LED reading lights, a dimly lit light above the bedframe, a small table LED lamp for reading and downlights at the entrance. Bedding was so so. Honestly I had expected more from a Novotel hotel.

reviews.title: Curb your enthusiasm
reviews.text: Unless you have specific business in this part of BKK, you're gonna find yourself a long way from the action. Nearest BTS is 900m. That said, the hotel is very average, as per the Novotel brand. The room had NO mirrors apart from in the bathroom above the sink. Slightly dingy. I woke up every morning smelling like Indian curry, I presume either the sheets were air dried near an Indian restaurant, or perhaps a previous guest had soiled the mattress, I couldn't really pin down where it was coming from. Best thing about the hotel is the Maggie Choo bar downstairs - pricey but worth a visit for the design, models, music and entertainment.

reviews.title: The most terrible experience ever
reviews.text:
I booked 2 rooms for 2 nights for my family trip in Bangkok.  I did not pay cheap price for the hotel and never could imagine that the hotel’d be in such awful condition. Nothing is like Novotel standard. We got the room 30 minutes late than regulations about check-in time even though we finished all documents at 12.00. Sitting like idiots in the lobby. More importantly, we are welcomed by a dirty bed wrap which is moldy black. The staffs in receptionist never said sorry after I gave the feedback . The Indian staffs were at least smiling, the Thai staffs are not friendly and helpful at all, except for only 1 receptionist who was there at 13.00 -14.00 on 1st May 2023. She is nice and helpful, the rest is so opposite. 
3 days- 2 nights  staying there but they never cleaned the room even though I reminded about that 3 times. They totally made me lose my patience :( 
I just know that I would never come back any hotels in Novotel hotel chain and also recommend my friend not to.

The room was so basic that there is no proper place to sit down and dine in the room.

I would recommend to stay at other Novotel hotels in Bangkok if you prefer Accor hospitality.  Otherwise, it is wiser to try other hotels which are value for money and convenient.

reviews.title: Not worth
reviews.text:
We stayed for 3 days and two nights. Our room came without any tooth brushes, slippers, etc. After housekeeping on the first night, they threw out our requested toothbrushes, and I had to request it again. No overheadlighting above the bed.
There is also no ventilation in the bathroom. So after a shower, the steam built up some water inside the hairdryer. We also had an issue with out mirror being that it came off, exposing electricals to steam.
The Silom location is awful if you need to travel far. Every time we requested  a taxi, it would be a minimum wait of 20 minutes due to dense traffic. I do feel a bit cheated since this is meant to be a 4 star hotel.

reviews.title: Basic. Basic.
reviews.text:
I guess this is par for the course for Novotel, but having just been to several similarly priced local hotels I just came away disappointed. The room was clean, if small, and the bed comfortable, if with a poor view but the hotel seemed severely lacking in the service department. 

A few points:
* When we arrived, in a taxi there was no porter, or welcome of any kind. 
* I ordered room service at about 11:30pm, was promised my meal in 20 minutes then promptly heard nothing further about it. 
* The shower had an faulty electronic blind so a regular curtain had been added as well. There was no note of this just a set of unlabelled controls that trigger some slightly terrifying noises.

Re: the area. We couldn't didn't find the few restaurants around this area particularly appealing, but I guess there's that one with the "traditional" dancing you can ask reception about.

reviews.title: Old and Musty Smell
reviews.text:
This was probably one of my worst hotel experiences (and only to date). I travel frequently for both work and leisure, often solo, and I’m generally not very picky with hotels. As long as I have a clean and comfortable place to sleep and shower, I’m usually satisfied since I spend most of my time outside.

Unfortunately, my experience here was really disappointing. The room had a very strong musty smell that became uncomfortable throughout the night. As someone with asthma, it genuinely affected my breathing and made me concerned about possible mold or poor ventilation in the room. I slept with a mask on.

On top of that, the walls seemed extremely thin. I could clearly hear noise from the neighboring room late at night, including conversations and someone getting sick, which felt unsettling especially as a solo traveler trying to rest before an early flight.

I expected better overall quality and comfort given the Hilton brand.

reviews.title: Extremely basic and not up to date with current times
reviews.text:
The rooms offer only very basic amenities. It’s quite an old hotel — although some maintenance work has been done, the facilities are still outdated.
For instance, the switchboards and TV setup are quite outdated. The TV still had the old-style audio and video jacks on the wall and wasn’t compatible with any OTT platforms like Netflix. We couldn’t even cast from our devices and had to stick to watching regular TV channels the old-fashioned way.
Despite having enough space in the room, there was no mirror provided, so we had to use the one in the washroom. The washroom also lacked an exhaust fan, which made it less comfortable.

Breakfast spread was good

reviews.title: It's quite messy.
reviews.text:
The location is a 10-minute walk from BTS Surasak Station. It might feel a bit eerie at night, making it inconvenient to come and go, but there are always tuk-tuks in front of the hotel and Grab arrives quite well.

Although it's not newly built, I trusted Hilton and stayed here, but it feels not just old but really dirty and poorly maintained. The room itself was dark so I didn't notice, but when I opened the window in the morning, I saw that the pillow covers were yellow, and the carpet on the floor was fully stained. It's fortunate that the room lighting is dim... I can really eat on the street while inhaling exhaust, but this place is the real deal.

I’ve been to old hotels in other countries, but this place seems completely unmaintained. I plugged my charger into the outlet, and when I pulled it out, dust came with it... I didn't spend a lot of money, but how can it be this dirty and messy… The floor is so dirty and there are no slippers. Please make sure to bring your own slippers.

The good thing is that the room is on a high floor, so it’s relatively quiet. I got a view of the Holiday Inn, but the view across the way is probably the Shangri-La Hotel, so it sounds quite nice. You can enjoy fireworks from luxury hotels for free (?). 

Oh, the pool is super small and the water is a bit murky, plus there was a Chinese person spitting on the pool floor.

If it were managed well, it would be nice, but really... it feels poorly maintained. It's cheap, and since there are services like room service and laundry service, a lot of Chinese people come here.

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text:
I’ve been a loyal Hilton guest for many years—my stays in Malaysia and Phuket were consistently perfect. Sadly, my recent visit to the Hilton in Bangkok did not meet those same high expectations.
The room was noticeably smaller than what I’m accustomed to at Hilton. It felt cramped, with barely any space around the bed. Even more disappointing was the lack of a sofa—something that’s standard in other Hilton properties I’ve stayed at. It didn’t feel like the spacious, comfortable environment Hilton usually provides.
Without a sofa, there was no comfortable seating area to relax or work in the room. All I had was the bed and a desk chair, which is hardly a substitute. This is not the experience I expect from a brand like Hilton
On a brighter note, the breakfast staff were exceptional—attentive, polite, and doing their best to make guests feel welcome. They were truly the highlight of my stay.

This is a trendy hotel that will probably appeal to couples and those attracted to the modern design. There is a screen in the shower that rises and lowers -- very sexy for couples. They also have free internet in the lobby. That was a big plus. 

However, there are some aspects that are a little irritating, such as a sloping sink where the faucet is offset and spills all over the place. Also, the double bed room was very small compared to most Bangkok hotels. I thought I was in Tokyo for a moment. It was that tiny.

Given the flaws, I would not stay again unless it was a real bargain -- which in my view would be $30 a night. Frankly, I expected a bit more from Novotel.

reviews.title: Advertised as 4 star but actually closer to a 3 star experience
reviews.text:
The staff is kind and accommodating but the hotel is quite old and dirty.  The room was spacious but smelled as tho it had been recently smoked in even tho it was a non-smoking room.  The carpet was old and dirty, the bathroom and shower head had scum and mold in various areas (vanity mirror, shower hand sprayer/shower head and automatic shower blinds).  It was comfortable for my needs and certainly an upgrade from the place I was staying before moving here but it’s certainly far from what was expected via the pics online.  Unfortunately in the online pics you can’t zoom in to see the dirtiness in the details.  

I enjoyed using the sauna, it was spacious and empty every time I went but it’s on an automatic timer that turns the temperature way down after some time, so if you show up and it’s been empty the temperature won’t be hot and you’ll have to turn it up yourself on the control.  It likely will take about 30 mins to reach peak sauna temps.  Also, the lockers in the men’s locker room did not lock.  

There was self serve drinking water in the lobby but it wasn’t provided in the room.

The bedding was comfortable and more than spacious for my needs.  

I still think staying there was a good value it’s just not as well kept as I would expect from a place that calls itself a 4 star hotel.  So if you’re the type that gets grossed out when they start to look in all the nooks and crannies then either don’t do that when you stay here or book somewhere else bc you won’t be happy with some of the hidden filth that you will find.

reviews.title: Clueless male front desk staff
reviews.text:
I recently had the opportunity to stay at Novotel Bangkok Silom Road from May 14th to May 15th, 2023. Overall, my experience at the hotel was positive, with a few minor drawbacks.

One of the standout aspects of the hotel was the size and cleanliness of the room. The room provided was spacious, allowing for ample movement and relaxation. The cleanliness was commendable, with a well-maintained and organized environment that contributed to a comfortable stay. The attention to detail in maintaining cleanliness was evident, and it added to the overall satisfaction of the accommodation.

However, I encountered a small inconvenience during my stay. There was one male staff member who appeared to be a bit clueless and unaware of certain guest requests. Even after my second check-in, during which I explicitly mentioned that I needed to go to the hospital first, this staff member failed to recognize my situation. It was disappointing not to receive the expected level of attentiveness and understanding from this particular staff member. This lapse in service left a somewhat negative impression on an otherwise pleasant stay.

Despite this isolated incident, the overall experience at Novotel Bangkok Silom Road was enjoyable. The hotel's location was convenient, with easy access to nearby attractions and amenities. The facilities provided by the hotel, such as the dining options and recreational areas, were satisfactory and added value to the stay. The hotel staff, apart from the aforementioned individual, were generally attentive and helpful.

In conclusion, Novotel Bangkok Silom Road offers spacious and clean rooms, making for a comfortable stay. While the professionalism of one male staff member left something to be desired, the hotel's overall service, location, and amenities make it a worthwhile choice for visitors to Bangkok.

reviews.title: Hotel with great price but few amenities
reviews.text: Overall, Novotel was a good decision after all. If not for the points that I have redeemed with the price I paid, I wouldn't say it was really worth it. The room we booked came with complimentary mini bar but selection was horrible. The room was elegantly designed, and was big enough for the two of us. I like how they put "magic" mirrors in the toilet that was being translucent with a switch of a button. Buffet breakfast was a disaster. Choices were really poor. Was on a high protein diet so I found the meats were nowhere to be found aside from the chicken sausage they had. Only upside is that staff were friendly and great. There was also supposed to be a dedicated check-in/check-out for premiere floor guests but it was nowhere to be found.

reviews.title: The least enjoyable of a series of stays
reviews.text:
I've stayed at Narai three or four times now, and this was the least pleasant of them all. Although basic features of the hotel have not changed -- location, reasonably priced, even in season, and service -- the room I had this time was as basic as it gets. The shower barely worked as all the fixtures were loose. Also, the hotel's housekeeping staff continues to keep its phones in the hallway while cleaning, and so every time a guest checks out on a particular floor, the phone rings to have the room checked. This is not at all conducive to peaceful sleep beyond 9 am. 

With the opening of the Novotel Bangkok Fenix Silom Hotel down the street, provided that the price level remains similar, I don't expect to stay at the Narai again.
reviews.positives: Reasonably priced hotel in a nice neighborhood
reviews.negatives: Old old old.

Aside from being clean, this hotel has not much going for them.

Being a 4star Hotel, you should have facilities/amenities and services on point. In this hotels case, the service is ridiculous. When my wife and I arrived (early and already informed in advance), the front desk staff told us it would take 2hrs to clean the room since the previous guests have “just checked out”. This is a little inconvenient, since we already called in advance and confirmed our early check-in, which was told to us that it was approved. Secondly, after waiting the 2hrs for their slow housekeeping to clean the 1 room out, another staff told us it would take another 45mins to and hour. It absurd that you have guests waiting in the lobby like zombies after long and early flights, and even then your services are not up to the mark!

After finally reaching our room, we felt more deceived - booked a “Superior King Room”, which entails a king size room, king size bed and king size bathroom. Let me telyou, there

reviews.title: Disappointed To Be Disappointed
reviews.text:
There are many things,  mostly location and brand name,  to recommend this hotel.

My room on the 17th floor had a faulty air-conditioning valve which on the first night held the temperature at (for me) a very cold 19°C.   The duty manager,   Khun Oum,  made three attempts to fix it on my first night finishing at a frustrating 1:30AM (the hotel was full).

I made a real fuss about their promises that it was fixed when it wasn't on the second day and they changed the air-conditioning valve.   Because it was pre-paid via Agoda,  no refunds were available despite the fact that I slept in my clothes (!) to stay warm.

To me,  this is just poor management which when a problem develops should fix it the first time in a 200+ room hotel with this Accor brand name.    

A second issue is that the hotel sits at the intersection of two major Bangkok roads and an overpass highway.  If constant street noise bothers you,   the noise footprint is higher than many central BKK hotels.

reviews.title: Cozy, Noisy, Strange.
reviews.text:
The first impress from the website was nice; High Class Hotel with a lot to offer, however some of the stuffs should have been mention.  As I am a light sleeper, I have requested for the upper level floor which I got Floor 18th which was nice and everything until.. late at night where you can clear hear the sound of the elevators clearly going up and down (first I was thinking that somebody was vacuuming in the middle of the night and they would stop soon (Nope, it was going on all night; and I didn't get any sleep over it) Second, some of the item were on request as I have heard from the other people checking in while I was checking out such as the toothbrush.  The Bedding and the Pillow was nice and comfortable, the A/C was very cool; however, it was a little loud, but can't compete with the elevators sound.  The Toilet where it was located separated to the bath and sink area was a nice touch; however, the toilet was not so clean, some of the smear was left out.  Unfortunately, the drain over at the shower was also clogged and I have to be-careful when taking a shower, as if was taken too long the whole bathroom will flush.

In conclusion, for the price I am paying, even when agoda have give some discount, I would rather pay more for a nicer sleep somewhere else.

On our special request some additional benefits were also thrown in for our celebrations.
The Premium Lounge is very good and the staff there was extra-ordinarily friendly and helpful. We were taken very good care by them. The hotel management has however decided to discontinue this facility, though we got it during our stay.
The buffet breakfast was usual, and had a good spread including vegetarian indian food.
However, I would like to highlight here that the general cleanliness was much below par. The crockery /cutlery was found to be unclean many a time during our stay.
The hotel massage spa was satisfactory
The location at the end of the road was something that we appreciated as it made it away from the usual rush seen on that road.
Overall our stay was pleasant and good

It was my second stay at this hotel which opened just for a year. Last experience was great since they free upgraded me to a primer suite.  Privilege to enjoy the late checkout and their executive lounge. And I'm so impressed by what and how I was served.

Stayed in a standard room this time, I did not expected to be anyone special though. I did not expect such a big difference on how the service is delivered.  Once a heavily raining evening, I stayed in and ordered room dining. It took me to repeat my order for six times to get the F&B staff to understand me.. and English indeed.  Then I waited 45minutes for a fish fillet  burger. Since there's a little placard reminds not to leave tray at room door. I followed the instruction to call F&B to collect my tray. Nobody answer the call. I called housekeeping, nobody answered the call. I called concierge and they would send someone immediately, I stayed awake for 2 hours and wait. nobody came and it ended up the tray left inside my room for the whole night.

No to mentioned the terrible design of rain shower which drains the whole washroom floor.

reviews.title: Forced 9 Day Stay
reviews.text: Don’t be fooled by the shiny Reception Area. The Pool area is very tired as is with most of the interior and exterior of this once Novotel. The Rooms are smaller and really meant.  for the Single Business Traveller. Housekeeping is a lottery whether you get a full quota of towels daily as often you have to ask for them and water. WiFi good but no instructions how to get on and the Reception Staff don’t seem to speak good English to explain. OVERPRICED, I’ve stayed in better Hotels with bigger rooms for less than half the price I’ve paid here.

reviews.title: Not Great but Not Bad
reviews.text:
Reading through all of the reviews, I was a bit worried about what I would find.  Having a conference booked at the nearby Shangri-la, but not wanting to spend a few hunderd dollars a night, I took my chances on the Hilton Garden Inn and don't regret it.  

When I arrived the room had a strong smell of fabric softner or perfume, making me wonder what it was hiding.  When it eventually wore off, there were some small smells from the bathroom drains, but nothing that affected my stay.  The room was small, but well laid out.  There were some stains on the headboard and the duvet cover, but it looked like the cleaners had tried to get it out and they were just part of the room now .

Location was good for walking to my meetings and to the BTS - lots of massage parlors near by if you want to relax, and the food in the restaurant wasn't bad either, although overpriced.

 But in no way is it a 4* with very small, averagely appointed rooms in a small footprint facility.
